title: Star Wars (1977) 1
content:

{{Quote|…Obi-Wan ''Kenobi''--help me! You're my only ''ho''--|Leia Organa|<ref name="Star Wars (1977) 1">[[Star Wars (1977) 1|''Star Wars'' (1977) 1]]</ref>}}
'''''Star Wars'' 1''' is the first issue of the [[Star Wars Legends|Legends]] [[Star Wars comic books|comic book]] series [[Star Wars (1977)|''Star Wars'']]. It was written by [[Roy Thomas]], based on a screenplay and story by [[George Lucas]], illustrated by [[Howard Chaykin]], and first published on [[April 12]], [[1977]]. The issue adapts the beginning of the film [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which premiered in theaters the following [[May]].

==Publisher's summary==
''It's the adaptation of'' A New Hope! ''Meet Luke Skywalker, Princess Leia, Obi-Wan and the villianous ''[''sic'']'' Darth Vader!''

==Opening crawl==
{{OpeningCrawl
|name=STAR WARS
|text=It is a period of CIVIL WAR in the galaxy.

A brave alliance of UNDERGROUND FREE-
DOM FIGHTERS has challenged the tyranny
and oppression of the awesome GALACTIC
EMPIRE.

To CRUSH the rebellion once and for
all, the EMPIRE is constructing a
sinister new BATTLE STATION.
Powerful enough to destroy an en-
tire planet, its COMPLETION will
spell CERTAIN DOOM for the
champions of freedom.

Striking from a fortress hidden among the billion
stars of the galaxy, REBEL SPACESHIPS have won
their first victory in a battle with the powerful
IMPERIAL STARFLEET. The Empire fears that
ANOTHER defeat could bring a THOUSAND MORE
solar systems into the rebellion, and IMPERIAL
CONTROL over the galaxy would be LOST FOREVER.}}

==Plot summary==
===A desperate mission===
[[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Forces]] led by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] capture [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa's]] ship, which is on a mission for the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]]. Two [[Droid/Legends|droids]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] and [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], manage to escape from the Rebel ship with data critical to the Princess' cause.

After arriving on the nearby [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], the droids are acquired by young moisture farmer [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and his uncle [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen Lars]] to work at their farm. Luke finds part of an urgent message from Leia to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] in R2's memory. R2 escapes from the farm with the intention of finding Kenobi on his own; Luke, fearing punishment from his uncle for losing the droid, pursues R2 to the [[Jundland Wastes/Legends|Jundland Wastes]], only to be attacked by a band of ferocious [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Sand People]].

==Release and reception==
While the issue was dated [[July]] on the cover,<ref name="Marvel July">{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/53791/star_wars_1977_1|text=Star Wars (1977) #1}} {{C|First printing}}</ref> it was in fact shipped on [[March 8]], and was made available on sale on [[April 12]].<ref name="YbY-New" />

In celebration of ''Star Wars'' Day on [[May 4]], [[2016]], Marvel re-released ''Star Wars 1'' as part of its ''True Believers'' budget reprint program, which reprints popular comic titles for the suggested retail price of $1.<ref name="Flickering Myth">{{WebCite|url=http://www.flickeringmyth.com/2016/03/marvel-celebrating-may-the-4th-with-true-believers-star-wars|text=Marvel celebrating May the 4th with True Believers: Star Wars|author=Cranswick, Amie|date=2016-03-25|archiveurl=https://archive.is/vtBEv}}</ref>

==Continuity==
The story in this issue includes a couple of Tatooine scenes that were cut from ''A New Hope'', including Luke Skywalker witnessing the opening space battle, Luke telling [[Camie Loneozner|Camie]] and [[Laze Loneozner/Legends|Fixer]] (who refer to Luke by his nickname "Wormie") what he saw, and [[Biggs Darklighter/Legends|Biggs Darklighter]] telling Luke of his plan to join the Rebellion.

The cover for this issue was based on [[Howard Chaykin]]'s original convention poster, which became one of the most valuable posters in the ''Star Wars'' collecting world. The artwork includes Obi-Wan Kenobi and [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], although they would not appear in the series until [[Star Wars (1977) 2|issue 2]], and [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wing]], [[BTL Y-wing starfighter/Legends|Y-wing]], and [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE]] fighters, which wouldn't appear in the series until [[Star Wars (1977) 5|issue 5]].

On the cover of both this issue and the following one, the first and last two letters of the ''Star Wars'' logo are not attached to each other.

==Legacy==
The comic includes the first reference to the concept of the [[Cosmic Force/Legends|Cosmic Force]]. Darth Vader's iconic line "The ability to destroy a planet is insignificant next to the power of the Force" is modified to specify "the Cosmic Force." Over 35 years later, "[[Voices]]," an installment of ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'', revealed the Cosmic Force's connection to the [[Living Force/Legends|Living Force]].
